# opnsense_quagga_ospf_neighbor (Resource)

Configure OSPF neighbors.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "opnsense_quagga_ospf_neighbor" "example" {
  address     = "192.0.2.1"
  description = "OSPF neighbor"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `address` (String) The neighbor IP address.

### Optional

- `description` (String) An optional description for this neighbor. Defaults to `""`.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable this OSPF neighbor. Defaults to `true`.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Poll interval in seconds. Use `-1` for unset. Defaults to `-1`.
- `priority` (Number) Neighbor priority. Use `-1` for unset. Defaults to `-1`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the OSPF neighbor.

## Import

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import opnsense_quagga_ospf_neighbor using the `id`. For example:

```terraform
import {
  to = opnsense_quagga_ospf_neighbor.example
  id = "<opnsense-resource-id>"
}
```

Using `terraform import`, import opnsense_quagga_ospf_neighbor using the `id`. For example:

```console
% terraform import opnsense_quagga_ospf_neighbor.example <opnsense-resource-id>
```
